ops.title: Stale mise GOROOT (go 1.26.4) vs newer homebrew go on PATH breaks any go compile/test with \"version does not match go tool version\"; env -u GOROOT fixes it
ops.body:
On this machine `GOROOT` is exported to a stale mise install (`~/.local/share/mise/installs/go/1.26.4`) while the active `go` binary is newer homebrew go (1.26.5+). Any Go compile — including Go-shelling tests like repo-bootstrap's `test_verify.py::test_end_to_end_go` — fails with `compile: version go1.26.4 does not match go tool version go1.26.5`.

**How to apply:** prefix `env -u GOROOT` on go builds/tests that hit the mismatch (seen live 2026-07-11, repo-bootstrap pytest: 194→195 green with it). Related: [[slop-cop-repo]] records the sibling broken-asdf workaround (`/opt/homebrew/bin/go`).
